prevalence.trend: stable; recognition higher in specialist trauma services

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria with longitudinal specialist assessment; differentiation from psychosis is essential

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with access to specialist trauma psychotherapy; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist trauma services more common in some member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Trauma-focused psychotherapy with stabilization and safety planning; treat comorbidities
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High service demand and access variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of specialist mental health services and structured psychotherapy in many systems
